mission: Build and maintain a serious, investor-grade public platform for Roofing Recyclers that explains the asphalt shingle recycling / RAS opportunity, builds trust across stakeholders, and supports future platform expansion.
primary_goals: Make the business model and RAS story immediately understandable.
Build credibility with investors, municipalities, manufacturers, contractors, and strategic partners.
Capture inbound leads through secure, structured forms.
Maintain an expandable architecture for future data, portal, certification, and content features.
secondary_goals: Support SEO, accessibility, and performance best practices.
Preserve premium visual quality on mobile and desktop.
Keep marketing copy centralized and easy to revise.
non_goals: Do not make the site feel like a local roofing contractor website.
Do not turn the public site into a fully built investor portal or blog engine yet.
Do not make unverified legal, policy, subsidy, or manufacturer claims.
hard_rules: Use Next.js App Router with TypeScript.
Use Supabase service-role access only on the server for lead inserts.
Preserve the industrial-tech, high-end visual direction and avoid cheesy eco or stock-template vibes.
Store durable project memory in docs/project-memory as canonical local-and-repo-backed memory.
strong_preferences: Centralize reusable marketing copy in content/site-content.ts.
Prefer reusable components over page-specific duplication.
Frame public copy carefully when discussing regulation, manufacturers, and policy momentum.
Deploy changes to production after meaningful UI or content fixes.
optional_preferences: Use real, relevant photos sparingly when they strengthen credibility.
Prepare routes and architecture for future expansion even when placeholders remain lightweight.
business_constraints: The site must speak to multiple stakeholder types without feeling generic.
Founder positioning matters and should communicate credibility without ego-heavy copy.
The public site should feel like the early face of a category-defining company.
technical_constraints: Stack is Next.js App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Framer Motion, Supabase, and Vercel.
Lead capture is backed by Supabase table public.inbound_leads.
Current typecheck flow depends on .next generated types and may require running build before a standalone typecheck.
compliance_notes: Do not store guesses as facts in public copy or memory files.
Use careful wording around regulations, incentives, exemptions, and specific commercial relationships unless directly confirmed.
Maintain accessible markup and readable contrast.
definitions_of_done: Requested changes are implemented in code.
Relevant project memory JSON files are updated with durable knowledge.
Build passes and typecheck passes in a valid sequence.
Production is redeployed when the change affects the live site.
